summary.rationale: The workflow surfaces that agents actually read now describe browser-first verification for visual UI work with explicit before-edit and after-edit expectations.

findings.message: The browser-first rule is now explicit in policy and agent guidance, but the supervisor runtime does not yet hard-fail UI runs that lack browser evidence.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a future supervisor or review-time gate if policy compliance proves inconsistent.

notes: The browser-first rule is explicit in the main operating manual.
The workflow remains additive and compatible with the existing phase model.
The sequence for visual work is clearer and easier to follow.
The verifier guidance now makes screenshot-backed browser evidence explicit.
The workflow now better matches how visual quality should actually be judged in practice.
The workflow language favors direct operational clarity over vague design-process advice.

next_steps: Consider runtime enforcement if browser-first verification is still skipped in practice.
